metadata.seed: I work and work and work but I can't seem to get ahead. The volume at work has gotten so bad that I can't keep up. I'm getting injured and no one cares. I can barely pay my bills and it feels like there is no end in sight.

patient.query: I work and work and work but I can't seem to get ahead. The volume at work has gotten so bad that I can't keep up. I'm getting injured and no one cares. I can barely pay my bills and it feels like there is no end in sight.

automatic_thoughts: there is no end in sight

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed by everything right now—work, injuries, and financial stress. Can you tell me more about how you're seeing the situation as having no end?

patient.query: Yeah, it just feels like I'm stuck in this loop. Every time I think things might be getting better, something else happens. Like, just when I start to get a handle on my job, I twist my ankle, and then the bills pile up. It's exhausting, and I can't see a way out.

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling really stuck, and those setbacks are making it hard to see progress. Can you tell me more about what makes you feel like this is a never-ending cycle?

patient.query: Honestly, it’s like every time I take a couple of steps forward, something knocks me back. I thought I was doing okay at work, but then I mess something up, and it just reminds me of all the times I’ve struggled before. It’s like I can’t escape this feeling that no matter how hard I try, I just won’t be good enough.

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling caught in a cycle where progress feels temporary, and setbacks reinforce past struggles. Can you tell me more about how those moments of mess-ups make you feel, and what evidence suggests you won't be good enough?

patient.query: It really feels like a punch to the gut when I mess up. It's like all the hard work I put in disappears in a flash. And the evidence? Well, there's that one time I missed a deadline and my boss wasn’t happy. I keep replaying it in my head, and it just makes me think that maybe I don’t belong there, you know? It’s hard to shake that feeling off.

behaviors: replaying the event in my head

llm_response.response: I hear how painful that feels for you. It sounds like those moments are really weighing on you and making you question your place. It makes sense that you'd replay it, especially when it feels so significant.

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel significant—the way I see it, it just proves what I fear deep down. I keep thinking about how everyone else seems to have it together while I’m just trying to keep my head above water. I know it might be irrational, but that comparison comes up every time I make a mistake. It’s hard to not feel like a failure.

automatic_thoughts: everyone else seems to have it together
I’m just trying to keep my head above water
that comparison comes up every time I make a mistake

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy weight when you compare yourself to others, and that must be incredibly difficult. When you mentioned everyone else seems to have it together, I wonder what you think they might actually be feeling or struggling with behind the scenes.

patient.query: It’s weird to think about, right? I guess they could have their own issues, but it just feels so real for me. I mean, when I look around, it seems like everyone else is just moving forward seamlessly. I don’t know if I can imagine them struggling like I am. It makes me feel even more isolated, like I’m the only one who can’t figure it out.

automatic_thoughts: everyone else is moving forward seamlessly
I’m the only one who can’t figure it out

llm_response.response: It makes sense that you'd feel that way when you're comparing your experience to others. When you look around, do you ever wonder what others might be thinking or feeling about their own challenges?

patient.query: Sometimes, but it’s hard not to feel like I’m just imagining it. I mean, part of me knows everyone has their own struggles, but that voice in my head tells me I’m the only one who can’t handle it. It's like I’m stuck in this bubble where my worries feel louder than anything else. I wonder if maybe they wouldn’t even understand how tough it feels when things go wrong.

automatic_thoughts: I’m the only one who can’t handle it
My worries feel louder than anything else
They wouldn’t even understand how tough it feels

entailment: Able to recognize own emotional pain and distress (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like there's a persistent inner voice that feels overwhelming, and it's hard to distinguish between reality and your own thoughts. Can you tell me more about how that "voice in your head" feels to you?

patient.query: Yeah, that voice is just... harsh. It’s like this constant critic that never stops talking. It reminds me of all my past mistakes, like a broken record, and I can’t escape it. Sometimes it feels heavy, like a weight pushing down on me, and it makes everything seem more daunting. It’s exhausting trying to fight it off, but I don’t really know how to quiet it down.

entailment: Tends to turn anger against self (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: That sounds incredibly difficult to carry around internally. When you mention it feeling like a weight, I wonder if that critic might be trying to protect you in some way, even if it's in a harsh manner?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I can see that. It’s just frustrating because the way it protects me feels so negative. Like, it’s always ready to jump in and remind me of everything I’ve done wrong instead of lifting me up. I mean, I want to be better, but it just feels like I’m being held back by that constant reminder. Sometimes I just wish it would let me breathe for a moment.

automatic_thoughts: reminds me of everything I’ve done wrong
behaviors: wish it would let me breathe

llm_response.response: I hear how frustrating it is when your inner critic feels overwhelming and restrictive. It sounds like you're longing for a moment of peace from its constant presence.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is overwhelming. I just want to feel like I can catch a break without all those reminders piling up on me. It’s like I can’t even enjoy the little victories because that voice swoops in and says, "It doesn’t count; you could have done better." I just want to feel okay about where I am for once without that shadow hanging over me.

automatic_thoughts: It doesn’t count; you could have done better
behaviors: feeling okay about where I am

llm_response.response: I hear how exhausting it must be to constantly battle that inner critic. It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel like you're never quite good enough, even when you've succeeded. It makes sense that you're feeling overwhelmed by the pressure to always do better.
